previous_statement: LORENTZ COVARIANCE: Under Lorentz transformation, T transforms as a rank-2 contravariant tensor. Proof: p-alpha is a 4-vector, gamma-dot-beta is a 4-vector, phi is scalar, d-tau is Lorentz-invariant, abs-det-Lambda = 1. Verify that the distributional definition makes this manifest.
new_statement: LORENTZ COVARIANCE: Under Lorentz transformation x' = Λx, the distributional energy-momentum tensor transforms as a rank-2 contravariant tensor. Proof by explicit distributional computation: (1) Define the transformed worldline γ'(τ) = Λγ(τ) and transformed momentum p'^α(τ) = Λ^α_γ p^γ(τ), justified by τ being Lorentz-invariant and p^α = mγ̇^α transforming as a four-vector. (2) Define T'^{αβ} distributionally via ⟨T'^{αβ}, φ⟩ = Σ_j ∫ p'^α_j(τ) γ̇'^β_j(τ) φ(γ'_j(τ)) dτ. (3) Substitute p'^α = Λ^α_γ p^γ, γ̇'^β = Λ^β_δ γ̇^δ, and φ(γ'(τ)) = φ(Λγ(τ)) = (φ∘Λ)(γ(τ)). (4) Factor: = Λ^α_γ Λ^β_δ Σ_j ∫ p^γ_j γ̇^δ_j (φ∘Λ)(γ_j(τ)) dτ = Λ^α_γ Λ^β_δ ⟨T^{γδ}, φ∘Λ⟩. This is the standard distributional transformation law T'^{αβ} = Λ^α_γ Λ^β_δ (Λ^{-1})_* T^{γδ}. No δ^{(4)} or |det Λ| argument is needed.
